note: Per-peptide ABSOLUTE and RELATIVE contraindications. Only FDA-approved agents (GLP-1s, Tesamorelin, PT-141/Vyleesi, SS-31/FORZINITY) have label-established items; all others have NO formal contraindication list — their absolutes are prudent-convention and relatives are mechanism-based, marked (VERIFY) pending Dr. Falcon sign-off. Engine: absolute -> hard-stop screen; relative -> advisory layer. Full cited detail in protocols/contraindications.md.

absolute: Personal/family history of MTC — boxed warning
relative: Pancreatitis history — discontinue if suspected; do not restart if confirmed
Severe gastroparesis / severe GI motility disease
Volume depletion / CKD — AKI from GI losses

absolute: Personal/family history of MTC — boxed warning
Serious hypersensitivity to tirzepatide or excipients
relative: Acute/recent pancreatitis — discontinue if suspected; do not restart if confirmed
Pregnancy/planning — fetal harm; reduced oral-contraceptive efficacy (barrier/non-oral 4 wk after start & each dose increase)
Diabetic retinopathy — monitor with rapid glycemic improvement
Volume depletion / CKD — AKI from GI losses

retatrutide.note: Investigational — NOT FDA-approved (Phase 3). No label contraindication list; class-extrapolated.
absolute: Personal/family MTC or MEN 2 — class signal, not label-established (VERIFY)
relative: Tachyarrhythmia, uncontrolled arrhythmia, decompensated HF, ischemic heart disease — dose-dependent HR rise; arrhythmias 2-11% vs 2% placebo — baseline HR/ECG if cardiac risk (VERIFY)
Glucagon agonism may raise glucose/hepatic output — caution in brittle diabetes (VERIFY)

absolute: Serious hypersensitivity to liraglutide or excipients
relative: Pancreatitis history — discontinue if suspected; do not restart if confirmed
Renal impairment / volume depletion — AKI from GI losses

aod-9604.note: No formal contraindication list; FDA GRAS food-ingredient only, not a drug.
relative: Active malignancy — precautionary only; no IGF-1 stimulation so GH-axis cancer rationale is weak (VERIFY)
Active malignancy — precautionary; mixed preclinical signals (VERIFY)
Diabetes / glucose-lowering agents — AMPK/insulin sensitization; theoretical additive hypoglycemia; monitor glucose (VERIFY)

ss-31.note: NOW FDA-approved (FORZINITY, Barth syndrome, 2025) — metabolic/longevity use is OFF-LABEL.
absolute: Serious hypersensitivity to elamipretide or any excipient (FORZINITY label)
relative: Hypersensitivity reactions incl. serious allergic reactions — discontinue and seek care
Subcutaneous only — not approved for IV use
Benzyl-alcohol excipient: NOT for neonates (fatal gasping syndrome in low-birth-weight/preterm); avoid in pregnancy/neonates absent clear need
Pregnancy — limited human data (VERIFY for off-label use)
Active malignancy — precautionary in compounded longevity use; not a label contraindication (VERIFY)

sermorelin.note: Geref discontinued; compounded off-label. No current FDA therapeutic label.
absolute: Non-functional pituitary / disrupted hypothalamic-pituitary axis — won't respond (VERIFY, extrapolated from tesamorelin label)
relative: Fluid retention, edema, arthralgia, carpal tunnel (VERIFY)
Concurrent glucocorticoids may blunt GH response (VERIFY)

absolute: Non-functional / disrupted pituitary axis (VERIFY, extrapolated from tesamorelin label)
relative: Fluid retention/edema, arthralgia, carpal tunnel (DAC = sustained exposure) (VERIFY)

cjc-ipamorelin-blend.note: No FDA approval and NO human trials of the combination.
relative: Diabetes / insulin resistance — dual-pathway GH amplification; monitor glucose (VERIFY)

ipamorelin.note: No FDA approval; two completed Phase 2 GI trials (negative). Cleanest GHRP.

ghrp-2.note: No US therapeutic approval (diagnostic GH-stim agent abroad); no therapeutic contraindication list.
relative: Cortisol and prolactin elevation — caution in hypercortisolism/hyperprolactinemia (VERIFY)
Fluid retention/edema, carpal tunnel; OSA; retinopathy (VERIFY)

ghrp-6.note: No FDA approval; no human efficacy trials; no contraindication list.
relative: Marked appetite stimulation (strongest GHRP) — counsel on weight gain (VERIFY)
Fluid retention/edema, carpal tunnel; OSA; retinopathy (VERIFY)

hexarelin.note: No human therapeutic approval or efficacy trials; no contraindication list.
relative: Receptor desensitization / tachyphylaxis with continued dosing (VERIFY)
Cardiac CD36-binding activity (preclinical) — caution in cardiac disease (VERIFY)
Fluid retention/edema, carpal tunnel; OSA; retinopathy (VERIFY)

tesamorelin.note: FDA-approved (EGRIFTA) for HIV lipodystrophy; off-label anti-aging use not label-covered.
absolute: Disruption of hypothalamic-pituitary axis (hypophysectomy, hypopituitarism, pituitary tumor/surgery, head irradiation/trauma) — label
Active malignancy — must be inactive and treatment complete before starting — label
Known hypersensitivity to tesamorelin or excipients (e.g., mannitol) — label
relative: IGF-1 elevation — monitor; consider discontinuation with persistent elevations
Fluid retention — edema, arthralgia, carpal tunnel
Glucose intolerance / new or worsening diabetes — monitor
Acute critical illness — consider discontinuing (GH-class mortality signal)
Recurrent malignancy — discontinue if any evidence of recurrence

mk-677.note: Oral non-peptide ghrelin agonist; no FDA approval; no formal contraindication list.
relative: Congestive heart failure / fluid-retention risk — edema & CHF signals in older-adult trials; avoid in HF (VERIFY)
Diabetes / insulin resistance — reproducibly raises fasting glucose, reduces insulin sensitivity; monitor (documented)
Elevated IGF-1 — monitor; long-term safety unknown (VERIFY)

bpc-157.note: No formal contraindication list (not FDA-approved). 503A compounding status in review — placed in Category 2, removed Apr 15 2026 (not yet on the positive bulks list); PCAC review Jul 23–24 2026 (VERIFY).
absolute: Active or prior malignancy — pro-angiogenic; tumor-promotion hazard (VERIFY)
Undiagnosed mass / malignancy red-flag until worked up
relative: Do not use to mask an undiagnosed surgical lesion — image first (VERIFY)
Personal/family cancer history — current screening before start (VERIFY)
Keep courses short and goal-bound — long-term human safety absent

tb-500.note: No formal contraindication list; consumer fragment has no completed human trial.
absolute: Active or prior malignancy — actin-binding, pro-angiogenic/pro-migratory (VERIFY)
Undiagnosed mass / malignancy red-flag until worked up
relative: Personal/family cancer history — current screening; malignancy ROS at follow-up (VERIFY)
Conditions where unwanted cell migration/fibrosis could harm (e.g., proliferative retinopathy) (VERIFY)
Consumer fragment != full-length Tβ4 (RGN-259); long-term safety unknown

ghk-cu.note: No formal contraindication list for systemic use; strongest data topical.
relative: Chronic/high-dose systemic (SC) use — monitor serum copper/ceruloplasmin (VERIFY)
Personal/family cancer history — current screening before systemic use (VERIFY)
Evidence largely topical; injectable/systemic use empiric

thymosin-alpha-1.note: Marketed as Zadaxin in ~35 countries but NOT FDA-approved; no US-label contraindication list.
absolute: Transplant on immunosuppression — Th1 modulation -> rejection/graft-failure hazard (VERIFY)
Severe active autoimmune disease — flare risk (VERIFY)
relative: Milder/controlled autoimmune disease — monitor for flare; hold on flare (VERIFY)
Concurrent immunosuppressive therapy — potential antagonism; coordinate with specialist (VERIFY)
Immune modulator, not a generic booster — use against a defined immune problem
Active/unworked-up GI or skin inflammatory disease — adjunct only; do not substitute for IBD/derm work-up (VERIFY)
Active or recent serious infection — could blunt a needed immune response (VERIFY)

semax.note: No formal contraindication list; not FDA-approved.
relative: Acute psychiatric instability / mania / severe anxiety (VERIFY)

selank.note: No formal contraindication list; not FDA-approved.
relative: Concurrent benzodiazepines / CNS depressants — additive sedation theoretical (VERIFY)

cerebrolysin.note: Marketed EU/RU (not FDA-approved); some items from manufacturer labeling.
absolute: Hypersensitivity to product/excipients (porcine brain-derived; anaphylaxis reported)
relative: Epilepsy / lowered seizure threshold — slow infusion + monitoring (VERIFY)

dsip.note: No formal contraindication list; zero registered trials.
relative: Untreated OSA / undiagnosed primary sleep disorder — don't mask (VERIFY)

epitalon.note: No formal contraindication list; zero registered trials.
absolute: Active or prior malignancy — precaution (telomerase/pineal mechanism; direction unproven) (VERIFY)
relative: Cancer history without current age-appropriate screening (VERIFY)
Pineal/melatonin-axis or circadian disorders; concurrent melatonin/hormonal therapy (VERIFY)
Use short pulses (10-20 d) — chronic safety unknown (VERIFY)

pt-141.note: FDA-approved (Vyleesi/bremelanotide) for HSDD in premenopausal women.
absolute: Hypersensitivity to bremelanotide or any component
relative: High cardiovascular risk — not recommended; assess CV risk and control BP
Dose limits: <=1 dose/24 h, <=8 doses/month (bound BP effect)
Oral naltrexone-containing products — may significantly decrease naltrexone levels; not recommended (VERIFY exact label wording)
Focal hyperpigmentation (face, gingiva, breasts) — more with daily dosing/darker skin; may not resolve

kisspeptin-10.note: Investigational; no FDA approval; mechanism-based (KISS1R agonist).
absolute: Hormone-sensitive malignancy (breast, prostate, endometrial, ovarian) — precautionary (VERIFY)
relative: Active fertility treatment / on gonadotropins — additive HPG stimulation (VERIFY)

gonadorelin.note: Brands (Factrel/Lutrepulse) historically FDA-approved but discontinued; US availability compounded only.
absolute: Conditions aggravated by rising gonadotropins/sex steroids (e.g., hormone-sensitive malignancy) — precautionary
relative: Pregnancy — use only if clearly needed (acceptable within supervised ovulation induction)
Pre-existing ovarian enlargement / OHSS risk — avoid; cyst rupture, ascites, thromboembolism
Pulsatile physiology is load-bearing — continuous exposure downregulates the axis

melanotan-ii.note: NOT FDA-approved; non-selective melanocortin agonist; items case-report-derived.
absolute: Melanoma or any pigmented/atypical/changing lesion (and personal melanoma history) — documented melanoma incl. oral mucosal after intranasal use (VERIFY)
Uncontrolled hypertension or significant CVD — documented hypertensive crises, renal infarction (VERIFY)
relative: Priapism risk — avoid/counsel in predisposed men (sickle cell, prior priapism); >4 h is a urologic emergency (VERIFY)
Severe nausea/vomiting; rhabdomyolysis (CK >5,000) and systemic toxicity reported (VERIFY)
Numerous/dysplastic nevi or melanoma family history — baseline + interval dermatologic exam (VERIFY)
